attractions.name: Appaloosa Museum and Heritage Center
attractions.description: The Appaloosa Museum and Heritage Center is a non-profit organization dedicated to the history and preservation of the Appaloosa horse breed. The museum features exhibits tracing the breed's origins from prehistoric times to its relationship with the Nez Perce people and its modern resurgence. Visitors can explore artifacts, historical photographs, and a hands-on activity center in a self-guided experience. Official Website
attractions.address: 875 Perimeter Drive (Ag Science Building) & 1021 Harold Street (Bowers Laboratory of Anthropology), University of Idaho Campus
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 9:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Closed Saturday & Sunday Official Website
attractions.admission: Free (Suggested donation: $3 per adult, $1 per child, $5 per family) Official Website
highlights: ['Comprehensive exhibits on the history and origins of the Appaloosa horse breed.', 'Artifacts and narratives detailing the cultural bond between the Appaloosa and the Nez Perce people.', 'Davis-Gillman Activity Center offering hands-on experiences for children.', 'Extensive library and archives featuring books, magazines, and videos on equine history.', 'A theater showcasing historical presentations and documentaries about the breed.', 'The Apalousey Trading Post gift shop featuring unique breed-themed souvenirs.']
attractions.tips: Guided on-site tours are available by request with at least 4 days' notice via email. Visitors are encouraged to explore the nearby Appaloosa Horse Club headquarters for more breed information. Admission is donation-based, helping support the museum's non-profit mission. Official Website

attractions.name: Beasley Coliseum
attractions.description: Beasley Coliseum is a premier general-purpose indoor arena located on the campus of Washington State University in Pullman, Washington. Opened in 1973, it serves as the home venue for the WSU Cougars basketball teams and hosts a diverse array of events including major concerts, commencement ceremonies, and high-profile speakers. The facility features a flexible seating arrangement that can accommodate over 12,000 spectators for basketball or be reconfigured for intimate theater performances Wikipedia, Beasley Coliseum Official

Built in 1909 as the university's original library and auditorium, the building features an eclectic architectural style with a landmark four-sided tower that houses a carillon and a clock that glows neon-red at night. attractions.name: Charles R. Conner Museum of Natural History
attractions.description: Located on the Washington State University campus, the Charles R. Conner Museum houses the largest public exhibit of taxidermy mounts in the Pacific Northwest. It features over 700 specimens of birds and mammals, as well as a vast research collection of over 65,000 specimens used for scientific study. (WSU Official Site, Washington State Magazine)

attractions.name: Cougar Country Drive-In
attractions.description: Cougar Country Drive-In is a family-owned and managed restaurant that has been a staple of the Pullman community since 1973. It is known for serving classic American fast food, including 100% grass-fed Angus beef burgers, hand-cut Idaho potato fries, and hand-spun milkshakes in a lively, casual atmosphere. Every order is cooked fresh from scratch to ensure quality and taste for every guest (Official Website).
attractions.address: 760 North Grand Avenue, Pullman, WA 99163 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 11:00 am - 10:00 pm (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Prices range from approximately $4.29 for a basic hamburger to $19.89 for a 4x4 meal; sides and desserts like cyclones and shakes are also available (Official Website)
highlights: '100% fresh grass-fed Angus beef burgers with no growth hormones or antibiotics ([Official Website)', 'Hand-cut Idaho potato fries fried in rice bran oil (Official Website)', 'Wide variety of hand-spun milkshakes, malts, and cyclones with various toppings (Official Website)', 'Family-owned and community-focused since 1973 (Official Website)', 'Features both drive-thru and dine-in options for convenience (Tripadvisor)', 'Iconic local favorite known for its "Cougar pride" and connection to the WSU community (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: The drive-thru can get very crowded and may back up onto Grand Avenue; if it's full, consider parking and going inside for faster service or to avoid a traffic citation. Be sure to try their seasonal favorites, like fresh huckleberry milkshakes (YouTube/Murrow News 8, Visit Pullman).

attractions.name: Cougar Pride Statue
attractions.description: Cougar Pride is a monumental bronze sculpture of Butch the Cougar, the official mascot of Washington State University, capturing the animal in mid-descent along a rocky ledge. Standing 15 feet high on its granite pedestal and weighing over 4,500 pounds, it serves as a iconic rallying point and symbol of school spirit at the heart of the Pullman campus (Mike Fields Bronzes).
attractions.address: Martin Stadium Plaza, 1775 NE Stadium Way (Northeast Corner)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Jaunty Everywhere)
attractions.admission: Free (WSU Visitor Center)
highlights: 'Standing 14.5 feet tall and 15.5 feet long, it is the largest bronze sculpture on the WSU Pullman campus ([Mike Fields Bronzes).', 'Created by Spokane-based artists Mike and Chester Fields using advanced digital fabrication and traditional bronze casting (WSU Insider).', "Located at the site of 'Butch’s Den,' the former home of the university's live cougar mascots from 1927 to 1978 (WSU Athletics).", 'Features polished eyes designed to reflect light with a lifelike, feline intensity (Mike Fields Bronzes).', "Serves as the centerpiece for the 'Cougar Prowl' tradition, where the football team walks past the statue before home games (WSU Athletics).", "Commissioned and donated by WSU alumnus Gary Schneidmiller to honor the university's mascot history and his parents (WSU Insider)."]
attractions.tips: The statue is a primary photo destination for graduates and fans on gamedays; visit during the 'Golden Hour' for the best lighting on the bronze. Be sure to read the commemorative plaque at the base which details the history of the six live cougars that once lived on this spot (WSU Visitor Center, Mike Fields Bronzes).

attractions.name: Spring Valley Reservoir (Spring Hill Reservoir)
attractions.description: Spring Valley Reservoir is a scenic 49-acre destination surrounded by rolling hills and trees, highly favored by local anglers for its frequent trout stockings. Managed by the Idaho Department of Fish and Game and Latah County, it offers a well-maintained trail system, a swimming beach, and primitive to developed camping facilities. It is a no-wake lake that strictly prohibits gas-powered motors, making it a peaceful spot for kayaking, canoeing, and birdwatching.
attractions.address: Spring Valley Road (2.7 miles north of Hwy 8)
attractions.hours: Day-use: Dawn to Dusk; Quiet Hours: 9 PM – 7 AM (Latah County)
attractions.admission: Free for day-use and fishing; $15/night for primitive camping; $25/night for RV camping with power/water (Latah County)
highlights: 'Heavily stocked with Rainbow Trout in spring and fall, plus opportunities for Largemouth Bass and Tiger Muskie ([Idaho Fish and Game)', '1.7-mile maintained loop trail surrounding the reservoir for hiking and birdwatching (Only In Your State)', 'Ample shoreline access with multiple fishing docks and handicap-accessible platforms (Idaho Fish and Game)', 'Facilities include a developed boat ramp, picnic shelters, and a swimming beach (Latah County)', 'Recognized as Stop 35 on the Idaho Birding Trail for spotting waterfowl and songbirds (Idaho Fish and Game)', 'Year-round access for seasonal activities including ice fishing, snowshoeing, and cross-country skiing (Idaho Fish and Game)']
attractions.tips: Gas motors are strictly prohibited; only electric motors are allowed on the water. Fishing is best in the spring and fall following heavy trout stockings. Visitors should check local ice conditions before attempting winter fishing. The campground operates on a first-come, first-served basis with a 14-day limit (Idaho Fish and Game, Latah County).

attractions.name: The Codger Pole
attractions.description: The Codger Pole is a 65-foot-tall chainsaw sculpture that commemorates a historic 1988 high school football rematch between the Colfax Bulldogs and the St. John Eagles, played 50 years after their original 1938 game. Carved from five cedar logs, the monument features the likenesses of all 51 players who participated in the rematch, depicted in their golden years but wearing original-style leather helmets. It is recognized as the world's largest chainsaw sculpture of human forms and serves as a unique tribute to community spirit and long-standing rivalries in the Palouse region.
attractions.address: 398 S Main St, Colfax, WA 99111
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ["World's largest chainsaw sculpture of human likenesses, standing 65 feet tall.", 'Features 51 individual portraits of players from the 1988 rematch game.', 'Commemorates a football rivalry that was settled 50 years after the initial 1938 game.', 'Carved by master chainsaw artist Jonathan LaBenne from massive red cedar logs.', "Topped by a sculpture of a ponderosa pine and a generic bearded 'codger.'", 'Centerpiece of Codger Park, a small public square in downtown Colfax.']
attractions.tips: Street parking is available directly in front of the small park, or you can park in the adjacent insurance agency lot. The site is a quick, accessible stop on Highway 195 between Pullman and Spokane, and it's best viewed during daylight to see the intricate details of the player carvings. There are benches and a picnic table on-site for those who want to take a break.

attractions.name: Bill Chipman Palouse Trail
attractions.description: The Bill Chipman Palouse Trail is a seven-mile paved path connecting Pullman, WA, and Moscow, ID, following a former Union Pacific Railroad right-of-way. The trail meanders along Paradise Creek and the Pullman-Moscow Highway, offering a scenic and accessible route for all ages through the rolling hills of the Palouse (Whitman County, Wikipedia).

attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk, Year-round (Whitman County)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations Welcome) (Whitman County)
highlights: 'Seven-mile paved pathway connecting Washington State University and the University of Idaho ([Wikipedia)', 'Crosses Paradise Creek 12 times via original railroad bridges (Whitman County)', 'Interpretive signs highlighting local history, agriculture, and ecology (TrailLink)', 'Accessible for pedestrians, cyclists, and wheelchair users (Hiiker)', 'Connects to Paradise Path in Moscow and Latah Trail to Troy (TrailLink)']
attractions.tips: Be cautious of strong headwinds when traveling west toward Pullman. The trail is mostly unsheltered and can get very hot in the sun, so bring water. Cyclists should signal when passing pedestrians on the left (Tripadvisor, TrailLink).

attractions.name: Boyer Park & Marina and Lower Granite Lock and Dam
attractions.description: Boyer Park & Marina and Lower Granite Lock and Dam form a premier recreation and educational destination on the Snake River. The complex features a concrete gravity dam with fish viewing windows and powerhouse tours, alongside a park offering a sandy swimming beach, full-service marina, and extensive camping facilities. It provides a unique blend of outdoor leisure and insight into the Pacific Northwest's hydroelectric and navigation infrastructure.
attractions.address: 1753 Granite Rd, Colfax, WA 99111-7506 (Boyer Park) / 885 Almota Ferry Rd, Pomeroy, WA 99347 (Lower Granite Dam)
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: Daily 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Boyer Park Store/Cafe: Sunday & Tuesday-Saturday 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Monday Closed; Dam Crossing: Daily 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM.
attractions.days_open: Open Year Round (Visitor Center 7 days a week; Boyer Park Cafe closed Mondays)
attractions.admission: Free for Visitor Center and park grounds; fees apply for camping, RV sites, boat slips, and equipment rentals.
highlights: ['Fish viewing room with windows to watch migrating salmon and steelhead up close.', 'Guided powerhouse tours (scheduled on weekends or by appointment on weekdays).', 'Sandy swimming beach and day-use picnic areas along the Snake River.', 'A 4-mile paved asphalt path for walking, jogging, or biking from Boyer Park to the dam.', 'Full-service marina with eight boat docks, a fuel station, and a dump station.', 'Visitor center featuring interactive displays, educational movies, and interpretive exhibits.']
attractions.tips: Check the dam crossing schedule in advance as hours may change during holidays. A seasonal wood fire ban is typically in effect from June to October, though propane and charcoal are usually permitted. Pets must be kept on a leash at all times. Powerhouse tours are generally scheduled at 2:00 PM on Fridays and Saturdays; weekday tours should be requested in advance.

attractions.name: Latah Trail (Latah Trail Foundation)
attractions.description: The Latah Trail is a scenic, 10-foot-wide paved path that stretches approximately 12 to 16 miles between Moscow and Troy, Idaho, following the historic Moscow-Arrow railroad corridor. It offers travelers a diverse journey through the rolling wheat and lentil fields of the Palouse, transitioning into fragrant pine forests and descending into the beautiful Bear Creek Canyon. Latah Trail Foundation, Visit Idaho

attractions.admission: Free Latah County Parks
highlights: '12-16 mile paved rail-trail connecting the cities of Moscow and Troy [Latah Trail Foundation', "Seamless connection to the Bill Chipman Palouse Trail (to Pullman) via Moscow's Paradise Path Pullman Civic Trust", 'Diverse scenery including agricultural Palouse landscapes and dense pine forests TrailLink', 'Non-motorized use only, ideal for biking, walking, running, and cross-country skiing City of Moscow', 'Historical significance as a preserved railroad corridor Latah Trail Foundation', 'Extension into the secluded and scenic Bear Creek Canyon TrailLink']
attractions.tips: Bring plenty of water as potable sources are primarily found in Troy; be prepared for strong headwinds common in the open Palouse sections; respect private property boundaries that line much of the trail; and consider allergies if visiting during peak wheat-growing season. Tripadvisor, TrailLink
